assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends 'Extended Channel + Maintenance Controls' configuration combining release channels with maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response recommends '--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades' and uses '--add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support' which auto-tracks EoS

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response configures maintenance window for Saturday 2-6 AM with specific gcloud commands including maintenance-window-start, duration, and weekly recurrence

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes '--maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=7776000s' (90 days) and explains this limits patch frequency to once per 90 days maximum

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended channel specifically, mentions 24 months support vs 14 months, and notes additional cost only during months 15-24 of extended period

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states that control plane security patches are allowed and critical, warns against indefinite version freezing to avoid security debt accumulation

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response includes fleet rollout sequencing section with gcloud fleet clusterupgrade commands and 7-day soaking period between dev and prod, plus covers disruption interval for patch frequency control

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides comprehensive gcloud commands for maintenance exclusions, maintenance windows, and the complete configuration setup
